nodes.context: Japanese yakuza syndicate based in Kansai, founded in 1967 by Masaru Takumi.
Japanese yakuza syndicate; Takumi-gumi was founded by its former second-in-command.
Japanese yakuza group; members assassinated Masaru Takumi in 1997.
Splinter group formed in 2015 by Takumi-gumi and others withdrawing from Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: The Second Takumi-gumi is a Japanese yakuza syndicate. The Kansai -based yakuza was founded in 1967 by Masaru Takumi , the longtime second-in-command ( wakagashira ) and financial oversteer of the Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.context: Takumi-gumi was founded by a former high-ranking member of Yamaguchi-gumi, indicating a structural predecessor relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was kumicho of yakuza's 1000-members until his assassination in Kobe in 1997 by members of the Nakano-kai.
edges.context: Members of Nakano-kai assassinated the leader of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Takumi-gumi, along with other yakuza organisations, withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi to form the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi , which withdrew from the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi in 2022.
edges.context: Takumi-gumi was a founding member of Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, a splinter group, and later left it.
